summary: Python version 3.15.0 alpha 7 is now available. This release includes build instructions, testing methods, and detailed documentation on new features and changes. The developers emphasize the importance of profile-guided optimization (PGO) and link-time optimization (LTO) for performance improvements.

methods_tools_patterns.name: Profile Guided Optimization
methods_tools_patterns.description: PGO utilizes GCC or Clang to optimize the build process through profiling information.
methods_tools_patterns.evidence: PGO takes advantage of recent versions of the GCC or Clang compilers.

methods_tools_patterns.name: Link Time Optimization
methods_tools_patterns.description: LTO enables optimization across file boundaries for better performance.
methods_tools_patterns.evidence: LTO takes advantage of the ability of recent compiler toolchains to optimize across the otherwise arbitrary `.o` file boundary.

key_claims.claim: Python 3.15.0 alpha 7 introduces several new features and optimizations.
key_claims.evidence: We have a comprehensive overview of the changes in the `What's new in Python 3.15` document.

key_claims.claim: Documentation is regularly updated and available in multiple formats.
key_claims.evidence: Documentation for Python 3.15 is online, updated daily.

hype_signals: optimized build process will perform the following steps
Python binary that is optimized; suitable for distribution or production installation
open_questions: What specific new features are included in this alpha release?
Are there additional dependencies required for various Linux distributions that are not explicitly mentioned?
